reason: The item cleanly targets boundary-aware hazard interpretation from limited test metrics (HRR, venting velocity, FED) and rewards resisting unjustified system-level or mechanistic causal leaps. Decoys are largely plausible and hinge on identifiable single faults, though B/E partially overlap in the same comparison axis and some options rely on qualitative phrasing that can invite minor interpretive latitude, limiting discriminative sharpness.

The item cleanly targets boundary-aware evaluation of whether cell-level, open-condition FED/venting observations justify a broad operational safety claim, and the two correct options appropriately separate mechanical vs asphyxiant inference limits. Decoys are mostly single-fault and plausible (scope overreach, causal overclaim, threshold misuse), producing good expert discrimination. Minor dilution comes from having two correct answers with overlapping “missing enclosure/ventilation context” logic and some options being easier to spot via absolute-threshold/wording cues.

The prompt tightly anchors admissible conclusions to specific reported metrics (pressure, HRR with/without secondary ignition, FED) while explicitly requiring boundary-aware limitations for system-level recommendations. It strongly discriminates between candidates who can integrate multi-hazard trends and those who overgeneralize cell-level/open-condition data, though the short-answer format reduces control over single-fault decoy behavior and can admit partially correct but incomplete responses.
